title: [EPIC] Deterministic evaluator & operator realisation
description: SPEC ¬ßEvaluation model, ¬ßModes, ¬ßConfig operators. Bottom-up evaluator + the non-LLM realisation strategies so the whole `tests:` block passes offline. Child scope (label `eval`): DAG evaluator (operand-first); realisation resolution (`command`/`reduce`/`template`/`join`/`model`) + mode selection; template realisation (`%`, `%0`/`%1`, `%%`); join realisation (variadic); numeric `reduce` builtins (add/sub/mul/div/pow); command realisation (`NLIR_ARGS`, stdout); operand type coercion before apply; list rendering via `_sep` + spread into variadic ops; nullary-pop stack consumption.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Project skeleton: Nix flake + Rust `nlir` CLI with crate deps wired
description:
Foundational bead. `SPEC.md` at the repo root is the normative contract for the whole project ‚Äî read it first; all beads implement it.

Set up the initial project scaffolding so every downstream bead has somewhere to land:

- **Nix flake** managing a Rust CLI, following the same pattern as the other harryaskham Rust CLIs. Use the **`omni-cli`** project as the primary reference for the flake + CLI structure (mature examples also live under `~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/‚Ä¶`, e.g. `tendril`, `kittui`).
- **Rust binary crate `nlir`** with the command-tree scaffolding and stub subcommands from SPEC ¬ßCLI surface: `-e`, `parse`, `test`, `repl`, `set`, `get`, `append-message`, `mcp stdio`, `self-update`, `feedback`, plus global flags (`--config`, `--context-file`, `--session-file`, `--mode`, `--model`, `--parallelism`, `--quiet`, `--dry-run`).
- **Wire crate dependencies on the friend-project template stack** (these are configured Cacophony friend projects / sub-crates):
  - `mcp-cli` ‚Äî gives the MCP server almost for free (`nlir mcp stdio`).
  - `updatable-cli` ‚Äî `nlir self-update`.
  - `feedback-cli` ‚Äî `nlir feedback`.
  Reference how `omni-cli` consumes these three crates and mirror that wiring.

Acceptance: `nix build`, `nix flake check`, and `cargo build` are green; `nlir --help` lists the command tree; the three template subcommands are wired (even if their handlers are thin) so later beads only fill in behaviour.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Context store, stack & assignment
description: SPEC ¬ßRuntime state, ¬ßBuiltins (stack + context). The two-namespace runtime: JSON context KV + internal stack. Child scope (label `context`): context load/merge (`NLIR_CONTEXT` env / `--context-file` / default `~/.config/nlir/context.json`); system keys (`_messages`, `_sep`=`\n`, `_cache`=true); stack machine (`;` push, `$`/`$N` peek, nullary-pop); `key=RHS` assignment (literal key, immediate write-through); `$name` read + greedy eval-time resolution; `"‚Ä¶"` `$name` interpolation.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Message indexing (^ role views)
description: SPEC ¬ßBuiltins (Message indexing). `^` as sugar over `$_messages`, role-filtered. Child scope (label `messages`): role views `^`=assistant / `^_`=user / `^*`=all / `^/`=system (config-driven role mapping); index resolution where indices are expressions coerced to number, negatives from the end; range `M^N` (and variants) with contents joined by `_sep`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Type system & coercion
description: SPEC ¬ßTypes & coercion. Tiny type system (`string`/`number`/`bool`/`list`) with per-position coercion. Child scope (label `types`): typed value model; deterministic coercions (number‚Üîstring, list‚Üístring via `_sep`, string‚Üíbool); LLM coercion fallback ("interpret as type T" with structured-output schema, config-driven under `types:`); loud coercion errors + `list‚Üínumber` is an error; coercion caching under `_cache`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Parser & DAG construction
description: SPEC ¬ßMental model, ¬ßGrammar & parsing. Precedence-climbing (Pratt) parser driven by config priority/fixity, producing a DAG of statements. Child scope (label `parser`): Pratt core; prefix/postfix/infix + arity; variadic flattening; mixfix unification (infix / prefix-on-list / postfix-on-list / nullary-pop); grouping `(‚Ä¶)` + list literals `[a,b,c]`; statement split `;` + DAG build; backtick serial-marker (low-precedence prefix over whole RHS); `nlir parse` AST/DAG dump.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Config loading, schema & validation
description: SPEC ¬ßModes, ¬ßTypes & coercion, ¬ßExample config.yaml. The `~/.config/nlir/config.yaml` layer that defines the whole language (models, prompts, operators, context, sessions, types, defaults, tests). Child scope (label `config`): serde schema structs; config discovery + load with `--config` override and default path; OS-env interpolation at load (`$FOO`/`${FOO}`); validation (required fields, unknown keys, operator op/arity/fixity sanity, reserved-sigil collisions); defaults resolution (mode/model/parallelism, context `_sep`/`_cache`).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] LLM model backends & prompt pipeline
description: SPEC ¬ßPrompt templating pipeline, ¬ßExample config (models/prompts). The `llm`-mode realisation. Child scope (label `llm`): model resolution (alias‚Üímodel, default model); `anthropic_messages` backend (direct HTTP, `json_schema` structured output, `result_field`); `command` backend (subprocess, `format` json/text, `result_field`); prompt fragments (`prompts:` env-named); `%` operand substitution (arity-1 `<text>`, variadic `<text n=k>`, `%%`); `${NLIR_*}` assembly (`NLIR_PROMPT` + system/structured/unstructured + `NLIR_ARGS`); result extraction (json `result_field` vs raw stdout text).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Lexer / tokeniser
description: SPEC ¬ßWhitespace/escapes/quoting, ¬ßGrammar (Tokens). Turn shorthand text into a token stream. Child scope (label `lexer`): non-semantic whitespace stripping between tokens; bare `[a-zA-Z0-9]+` and numeric literals; quoted literals `'‚Ä¶'` (raw) / `"‚Ä¶"` (escapes processed) with POSIX escapes (`\n \t \  \\ \" \'`); operator longest-match (`**` before `*`); builtin sigils `; $ ^ = [ ] , ( )` + backtick and sub-forms (`$name`/`$N`, `^`/`^_`/`^*`/`^/`, `key=`); role-modifier and negative-index disambiguation after `^`/`$`.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham
